Strong ties matter more for emotional support, lending money, crisis help. Modern: LinkedIn networks are mostly weak ties — useful for opportunities, less useful for personal support. The proverb names different tie strengths for different functions. One of network science's foundational findings.
wisdom: The friend you call when sick is rarely the one who tells you about the job opening. Both kinds of ties matter — for different things. The proverb is sociology's network-economics 101.

title: Labor unions raise wages — for members.
verification: Union wage premium: unionized workers earn ~10-20% more than equivalent non-unionized workers, controlling for skill and experience (Card et al. 1996; multiple replications). Effects: higher wages, better benefits, more job security; sometimes higher unemployment in industry (employers reluctant to hire), spillover effects on non-union wages in same region. US private-sector union membership peaked ~35% in 1955, now ~6%; public sector ~33%. Various proposed mechanisms for decline: legal changes (Taft-Hartley 1947, right-to-work laws), globalization, decline of manufacturing, anti-union litigation. The proverb names a real effect with declining scope as unionization itself declines.
wisdom: Collective bargaining works for those organized. Its scope has shrunk from a third of private workers to a tenth in two generations. The proverb still describes reality where unions exist.

title: Leap year every four — except century years not divisible by 400.
verification: Tropical year (Earth's orbit relative to sun): ~365.2422 days. Julian calendar (46 BCE): 365.25 days/year average (1 leap year per 4) — overshoots by ~11 minutes/year, accumulating to ~10 days by 1582. Gregorian reform (1582): drop 3 leap years per 400 — century years (1700, 1800, 1900) NOT leap, but 2000 IS leap. Average: 365.2425 days/year — accurate to ~26 seconds/year, ~1 day per 3,236 years. Year 4000 may need further adjustment (proposed). The proverb is calendar arithmetic that took human civilization 1,500 years to refine.
wisdom: Three exceptions inside one rule. The proverb is what kept Easter on the right date and what made Pope Gregory XIII a calendrical hero. Programming date arithmetic is one place programmers consistently err.

title: Easter falls on the first Sunday after the first full moon after equinox.
verification: Council of Nicaea (325) settled Easter date: first Sunday after first ecclesiastical full moon on or after March 21 (spring equinox). Ecclesiastical full moon uses simplified lunar calendar (epact tables) rather than astronomical full moon. Range: March 22 to April 25 in Western (Gregorian) Catholic/Protestant calendar. Eastern Orthodox uses Julian calendar reckoning → typically 1-5 weeks later. Combined astronomical + ecclesiastical + theological complexity makes Easter computation famously intricate (Gauss derived an explicit algorithm in 1800). The proverb is one of liturgy's most-asked questions.
wisdom: Solar equinox + lunar phase + Sunday. Three timekeepers combined. Easter's wandering date is the proverb's annual reminder that Christian time is built on multiple cycles.

title: Load path: a structure stands by what it transmits, not what it contains
verification: Every static structure must transmit gravity and lateral loads to ground through a continuous path. Break the chain anywhere and the structure collapses regardless of how stout individual members are.
wisdom: A chain of weak members can carry more than a strong member with a missing link. Audit the path, not the parts.

title: Tributary area determines column size, not floor area
verification: A column carries the load from the area whose load drains to it (tributary area), which is typically 1/4 of each bay it touches. Corner columns carry less than interior columns of the same building.
wisdom: Don't size all columns the same — interior ones do 4x the work of corner ones in regular grids.

title: Critical path is the schedule's spine, not the longest list of tasks
verification: CPM identifies the longest chain of dependent tasks; that chain determines project duration. Adding workers to non-critical tasks does not shorten the schedule.
wisdom: Speed up the critical path or accept the schedule. Manpower elsewhere is busy work.

title: Punch list close-out time is roughly 5% of total project duration
verification: Empirical rule from commercial construction; varies widely by complexity (1-15%). The pattern holds because final QA touches every system rather than scaling with size.
wisdom: Reserve budget and time for close-out separately — it doesn't compress.

title: Voltage drop limit of 3% per branch, 5% total, sets wire gauge
verification: NEC recommendation (informational) used as practical rule. Past these limits motors run hot, lights dim under load, electronics misbehave. Drop scales linearly with current and length, inversely with wire cross-section.
wisdom: Long runs need fatter wire than calculation from current alone suggests.

title: Real power = apparent power times power factor
verification: P (watts) = S (VA) * cos(phase angle). Inductive loads (motors) draw reactive power that does no work but loads the wires. Utilities charge industrial customers for poor PF.
wisdom: Capacitor banks correct PF; pays back fast in industrial settings.

title: Three-phase systems must be load-balanced or neutral carries the imbalance
verification: In balanced 3-phase, neutral current is near zero by vector sum. Imbalance forces neutral to carry the residual, which can exceed individual phase current if loads are nonlinear.
wisdom: Distribute single-phase loads across the three phases when wiring panels.

title: Six sigma quality means 3.4 defects per million opportunities (with 1.5σ shift)
verification: Process mean is assumed to drift 1.5σ over time, so six sigma capability yields 4.5σ effective, which from a normal distribution gives 3.4 ppm in the tail.
wisdom: The 1.5σ shift is not a typo — it accounts for long-term drift not visible in short-term studies.

title: Takt time = available time / customer demand sets the production drumbeat
verification: Lean manufacturing: takt is the pulse rate of demand. A line running faster than takt builds inventory; slower than takt falls behind.
wisdom: Match cycle time to takt — speed is the wrong optimization target.

title: OEE = availability × performance × quality; world-class is 85%
verification: Overall Equipment Effectiveness multiplies three losses; 85% means each factor is ~95%. Most plants run 40-60%.
wisdom: Multiplication makes 95×95×95 = 85.7 feel tight — small drops in any factor compound.

title: Myelinated axons conduct at 100 m/s; unmyelinated at 0.5-2 m/s
verification: Saltatory conduction in myelinated fibers jumps node to node, dramatically faster than continuous propagation in bare axons. Multiple sclerosis demyelinates and slows signals.
wisdom: Speed gradient explains why MS symptoms include slowed reflexes and coordination loss.

title: Human cortex has roughly 10^14 synapses across 10^11 neurons
verification: Each neuron averages ~1000-10000 synapses; with ~86 billion neurons in the brain, synaptic count enters the 10^14 range. Connectome research uses this as the baseline scale.
wisdom: Network structure (which connects to which) carries more information than the count alone.

title: Binocular vision critical period closes around age 7
verification: Hubel and Wiesel monocular-deprivation experiments mapped visual cortex plasticity windows. Strabismus untreated past the critical period leads to permanent amblyopia.
wisdom: Some neurodevelopmental windows close — treat early, not later.

title: Family-wise error rate at α=0.05 across n tests reaches 1-(0.95)^n
verification: With 20 independent tests at 0.05, FWER ≈ 1-0.358 = 0.64 — a 64% chance of at least one false positive. Bonferroni corrects by using α/n per test.
wisdom: Running many tests without correction guarantees false positives; the question is how many.

title: FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) controls expected proportion of false positives, not their existence
verification: FDR at q=0.05 means among declared discoveries, about 5% are expected to be false. Bonferroni at α=0.05 means probability of any false positive is 5%. FDR is less conservative for genomics/imaging.
wisdom: Pick FDR for screening, FWER for confirmatory tests — they answer different questions.

title: Garden of forking paths inflates Type I error even without explicit multiple tests
verification: Gelman & Loken: analyst could have chosen many analyses given the data, even if only one was actually run. Pre-registration constrains the path; post-hoc choice does not.
wisdom: P-value is a property of a procedure, not a number — count the procedures you would have run, not the one you did.

title: Rank is the dimension of the column space, not the count of nonzero entries
verification: A matrix can be full of nonzeros and still rank 1 (rank-1 update). Rank is the count of linearly independent columns, equivalently rows.
wisdom: Dense matrices can be low-rank; sparse matrices can be full-rank. Look at structure, not entries.

title: Condition number predicts how much answer error you get per input error
verification: For Ax=b, relative error in x bounded by κ(A) times relative error in b. κ=10^k loses k digits. Hilbert matrices have κ growing exponentially in size.
wisdom: Ill-conditioned doesn't mean wrong matrix — it means amplified noise. Re-scale or use pseudoinverse.

title: First principal component is the direction of maximum variance, not maximum signal
verification: PCA maximizes variance, which equals signal only when noise is isotropic. If noise is anisotropic (typical in real data), the first PC can be dominated by noise direction.
wisdom: Pre-whitening (or using ICA) often beats raw PCA for source separation.

title: Unconformity in rock record means missing time, not faulted layers
verification: Erosion or non-deposition leaves a gap between rock layers. Hutton's Siccar Point shows 65My missing. Angular unconformities also tilt the lower sequence before the upper was laid.
wisdom: The record has holes — absence of rock is not absence of time.

title: U-Pb zircon dating's reliability rests on the mineral excluding lead at formation
verification: Zircon crystal structure rejects Pb but accepts U at crystallization, so any Pb measured later is daughter from U decay. Concordia plots from two U-Pb systems cross-check.
wisdom: The method works precisely because of zircon chemistry — not all minerals work.

title: Raw C-14 dates require calibration via tree-ring curves to become calendar dates
verification: Atmospheric C-14 varied across time; IntCal20 calibration curve maps radiocarbon years to calendar years using dendrochronology and varves. Uncalibrated dates can be off by centuries near plateaus.
wisdom: Report calibrated dates (cal BP) for any history claim; uncalibrated radiocarbon years are an intermediate.

title: Hallstatt plateau makes 800-400 BC dates imprecise regardless of method
verification: Atmospheric C-14 was nearly flat across 800-400 BC, so a single date in that range maps to a multi-century calendar range. Iron Age archaeology is uniquely cursed by this.
wisdom: Iron Age scholarship needs corroborating evidence (writing, stratigraphy) — radiocarbon alone is too coarse.

title: Chain of custody is broken by any unexplained gap, not just by tampering
verification: Federal Rules of Evidence: gaps in custody log raise inadmissibility risk even without evidence of alteration. The court can reject on procedure alone.
wisdom: Log every handoff; missing entries are as bad as proven tampering.

title: Office documents retain edit history, author IDs, and revision marks in metadata
verification: docProps/core.xml in .docx files exposes creator, last-modified-by, revision count. Track Changes can be hidden in view but lives in document.xml. Image EXIF carries GPS unless stripped.
wisdom: Strip metadata before sending PDFs/Office docs — or expect the recipient to see your file history.

title: Soil porosity controls drainage, root growth, and microbial activity simultaneously
verification: Bulk density and porosity govern how much air and water the soil holds. Compaction reduces porosity, suffocating roots and microbes; tillage destroys structure over time.
wisdom: Healthy soil is mostly empty space; compaction is the enemy of fertility.

title: Inclusion-exclusion alternates +/- across all intersection sizes
verification: |A ∪ B ∪ C| = |A|+|B|+|C| - |A∩B| - |A∩C| - |B∩C| + |A∩B∩C|. Generalizes to n sets with 2^n - 1 terms; signs alternate by subset size.
wisdom: Don't double-count; subtract the overlaps, then add back the triple-overlaps.

title: Coriolis deflects ocean currents right in N hemisphere, left in S
verification: Earth's rotation in a non-inertial frame produces apparent force perpendicular to velocity. Sets gyre direction, Ekman spiral, geostrophic balance for large-scale flows. Negligible for bathtubs.
wisdom: Coriolis dominates above ~1 km scale; bathtub-drain myth is bunk.

title: Circle of fifths emerges from frequency ratio 3:2 stacked twelve times
verification: Stacking 12 perfect fifths overshoots 7 octaves by the Pythagorean comma (~23.46 cents). Equal temperament flattens each fifth by ~2 cents to close the circle.
wisdom: The circle isn't a circle in pure tuning — it's a spiral. Temperament forces closure.

title: Aristotle's three modes of persuasion: ethos, pathos, logos
verification: Rhetoric I.2: ethos (character of speaker), pathos (emotion of audience), logos (reasoning of argument). Persuasion fails when any mode is missing. Most enduring framework in argumentation theory.
wisdom: A correct argument with bad ethos doesn't land; great ethos with bad logos collapses on inspection.

title: Strawman attacks a misrepresented version of the opponent's claim
verification: Informal fallacy: refute a weaker claim than was actually made. Common because the weaker claim is easier to defeat. Identifying requires close reading of the original argument.
wisdom: Steelman before responding — strongest version first, then engage.

title: Ad hominem attacks the person to discredit the argument
verification: Genuine ad hominem (attack on character irrelevant to argument) is fallacious. Circumstantial ad hominem (showing conflict of interest) can be legitimate when authority is being invoked.
wisdom: Attack the argument first; bring up character only when the argument relies on character.

title: Appeal to authority is valid only when the authority is relevant and credible
verification: Citing experts in their field is legitimate inference. Citing celebrities, out-of-field experts, or non-consensus authorities is fallacious appeal to authority.
wisdom: Ask: is this person an expert in *this* specific claim, and does consensus back them?

title: Loaded question smuggles a contested premise into the asking
verification: 'Have you stopped beating your wife?' presupposes prior beating. Both yes and no concede the premise. Defuse by rejecting the question, not answering it.
wisdom: Watch for embedded presuppositions in adversarial questioning.

title: False dichotomy presents two options as exhaustive when more exist
verification: 'Either with us or against us' collapses a continuum. Real options usually form a space, not a binary. Common in political and marketing rhetoric.
wisdom: List a third option explicitly — false dichotomies dissolve under enumeration.

title: Socratic method elicits reasoning by questioning, not by asserting
verification: Plato's dialogues show Socrates leading interlocutors to recognize their own contradictions via questions. The method assumes the answer is already latent in the respondent.
wisdom: Question-driven inquiry surfaces inconsistencies that direct argument cannot reach.

title: Principle of least privilege: grant the minimum access required for the task
verification: Saltzer & Schroeder 1975. Reduces blast radius of any compromise. Modern instances: scoped tokens, just-in-time admin, sandboxed processes, RBAC with deny-by-default.
wisdom: Default to no access; grant exactly what is needed; revoke when the task is done.

title: Defense in depth assumes any single layer will fail
verification: Layered controls (firewall + IDS + endpoint + segmentation + monitoring) require an attacker to bypass all layers. Single layer defense is brittle.
wisdom: Treat every layer as if it's the last line; in reality, hope it isn't.

title: Zero trust: never trust, always verify — including inside the perimeter
verification: NIST SP 800-207. Replaces perimeter model (castle and moat) with per-request authentication and authorization. Lateral movement protection.
wisdom: Internal network is no safer than external; treat all traffic as untrusted.

title: Phishing remains the #1 initial access vector — over 80% of breaches
verification: Verizon DBIR consistently reports social engineering as top initial access. Technical controls have improved faster than human ones.
wisdom: Train people and harden auth (MFA, FIDO2) — these are the highest-ROI defenses.

title: MFA blocks over 99% of automated account compromise attempts
verification: Microsoft and Google studies: MFA stops 99%+ of bulk credential-stuffing and password-spray attacks. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2) also defeats real-time phishing.
wisdom: Plain MFA stops bots; phishing-resistant MFA stops adversaries.

title: Isoglosses bundle to define dialect boundaries, but rarely align cleanly
verification: Dialectology maps individual features (lexical, phonetic, grammatical) as isoglosses. Major dialect lines (e.g., Rhenish fan) show bundles, but most isoglosses cross.
wisdom: Dialect borders are fuzzy gradients, not sharp lines.

title: Universal Grammar hypothesis: humans share innate language capacity
verification: Chomsky 1965. Cross-linguistic similarities and rapid child acquisition support innate constraints. Competing views (usage-based, emergentist) emphasize learning. Empirical debate ongoing.
wisdom: The poverty-of-stimulus argument is the strongest piece of UG evidence and still contested.

title: Strong Sapir-Whorf (language determines thought) is not supported
verification: Strong determinism falsified — speakers of all languages can think about and learn concepts their language lacks dedicated words for. Weak influence (linguistic relativity) shows small but measurable effects on perception/memory.
wisdom: Language shapes attention, not capacity. Don't overclaim from color-term studies.

title: Word frequency in any natural language follows Zipf's law: f ∝ 1/rank
verification: Empirically robust across languages and corpora. Top word ~2x second, ~3x third, etc. Mandelbrot generalization fits better at extremes.
wisdom: Top 100 words cover ~50% of text; covers the vocabulary explosion problem in NLP.

title: Golden section in classical architecture is partly retrofit, not always intended
verification: Many claims of φ in Parthenon, Pyramids, etc. are post-hoc — measurements close to 1.618 occur often when scanning many ratios. Le Corbusier's Modulor was explicit, but ancient use is contested.
wisdom: Beautiful ratios are easy to find after the fact; cite primary evidence before claiming intent.

title: Carnot efficiency 1 - Tc/Th is the absolute upper bound on heat engines
verification: Second law: no engine between two reservoirs can exceed (Th-Tc)/Th. Real engines are lower due to irreversibility. A 600K/300K engine maxes at 50% efficiency.
wisdom: You cannot beat Carnot by clever design; you can only approach it by reducing entropy production.

title: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium requires five conditions, all violated in nature
verification: Allele frequencies stay constant only with: random mating, no mutation, no selection, no migration, large N. Real populations violate all five but H-W is the null hypothesis against which evolution is detected.
wisdom: H-W deviation diagnoses evolutionary force — match the violation to the cause.

title: Linkage disequilibrium decays with recombination distance and generations
verification: LD = D = p(AB) - p(A)p(B). Each meiosis cuts D by a factor of (1-r). Strong LD localizes association signals near causal variants — basis of GWAS.
wisdom: LD is why GWAS tag SNPs work; LD blocks are why fine-mapping is hard.

title: Continental crust floats on mantle in isostatic balance — mountains have roots
verification: Lithosphere behaves like ice on water: Himalayas have ~70km roots while ocean basins have thin crust. Glacial loading depresses crust; rebound takes ~10,000 years post-melt.
wisdom: Tall mountains imply deep roots; thick ice depresses bedrock; both rebound on the same scale.

title: Stellar parallax method reaches only ~100 pc from Earth-based instruments
verification: Atmospheric seeing limits parallax precision to ~0.01 arcsec, giving 100pc range. Gaia spacecraft extends this to ~10,000pc with ~10 microarcsec precision.
wisdom: Distance ladder beyond parallax relies on standard candles, each step adds uncertainty.

title: Just intonation is mathematically pure but cannot transpose; equal temperament can transpose but is impure
verification: JI uses integer frequency ratios (5:4 major third). ET divides octave into 12 equal log-steps. JI sounds purer in one key but breaks when modulating; ET is uniformly slightly off.
wisdom: Keyboard instruments use ET; voice and strings drift toward JI when soloing.

title: PAdES digital signatures bind a hash of the PDF to a certificate
verification: PDF Advanced Electronic Signatures (ETSI EN 319 142): signature covers byte ranges except the signature field itself; any change after signing invalidates.
wisdom: A signed PDF that opens with 'signature is valid' is cryptographically verified — but the certificate's trust chain matters too.

title: AC current concentrates near conductor surface as frequency increases
verification: Skin depth δ = sqrt(2/(ωμσ)). At 60Hz in copper, δ ≈ 8.5mm. At 1GHz, δ ≈ 2µm. High-frequency transmission lines use hollow or Litz wire.
wisdom: Above ~10kHz, wire interior carries little current — no point in solid thick conductors.

title: Cation exchange capacity (CEC) measures soil's ability to hold nutrients
verification: Clay and organic matter carry negative charges that bind Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺, K⁺, NH₄⁺. CEC measured in meq/100g. Sandy soils ~5, loams 15-25, peat >100.
wisdom: Low CEC soils leach nutrients fast — split-apply fertilizer rather than dumping at once.

title: Replication crisis is partly driven by uncorrected multiple comparisons
verification: Ioannidis 2005, Open Science Collaboration 2015: only ~36% of psychology findings replicate. Major drivers: small N, p-hacking, undisclosed multiplicity, garden-of-forking-paths.
wisdom: Pre-registration + reasonable correction + adequate power are the antidotes.

title: Holm-Bonferroni is uniformly more powerful than Bonferroni at the same FWER
verification: Holm 1979: sort p-values, compare smallest to α/n, next to α/(n-1), etc. Stops at first non-rejection. Strictly dominates Bonferroni — same control, more rejections.
wisdom: Prefer Holm over Bonferroni when controlling FWER; there's no downside.

title: Quantum superposition: a qubit is in 0, 1, or both with complex amplitudes
verification: State |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩ with |α|²+|β|²=1. Measurement collapses to 0 with probability |α|². Bloch sphere represents pure states geometrically.
wisdom: Superposition is not 'both at once' — it's a vector in Hilbert space with measurement-dependent outcomes.

title: Entangled qubits exhibit correlations no classical system can reproduce
verification: Bell inequality violations (Aspect 1982, Hensen 2015 loophole-free) confirm non-local correlations. Cannot be explained by local hidden variables.
wisdom: Entanglement is a resource: teleportation, dense coding, and quantum key distribution all consume it.

title: No-cloning theorem: an unknown quantum state cannot be copied
verification: Wootters-Zurek 1982. Linearity of QM forbids a universal cloning operator. Foundation of quantum key distribution security.
wisdom: You can teleport a state but not duplicate it; this asymmetry powers quantum cryptography.

title: Shor factors 15 -- the quantum result, checked in two domains
situation: 15 was the first number ever factored on a quantum computer (IBM, 2001), by Shor's algorithm. The engine verified the whole result end to end. Shor reduces factoring to PERIOD-FINDING (the quantum part) plus a GCD (pure number theory). Pick a=2: the order of 2 modulo 15 is r=4, because 2^4 = 16 = 1 (mod 15), and r is even -- the quantum_computing verifier CONFIRMED this. Then the classical extraction: gcd(2^(r/2)-1, 15) = gcd(3,15) = 3 and gcd(2^(r/2)+1, 15) = gcd(5,15) = 5, both CONFIRMED by the number_theory verifier, which also CONFIRMED 3 and 5 are prime. So 15 = 3 x 5, HOLDS 5/5, sealed. A false period (r=3, odd, and 2^3 = 8 != 1 mod 15) claimed valid BROKE -- the engine refuses an invalid order.
use: Two things worth keeping. First, the famous quantum result is not taken on authority here -- it is re-derived and machine-checked, and would BREAK on a bad step. Second, it is a co-witness (Deuteronomy 19:15): the quantum domain supplies the period, the number-theory domain supplies the gcd and the primality, and they agree on the same factorization. That is the honest shape of Shor's algorithm -- the quantum computer finds the period; the rest is arithmetic anyone can check. The mystique is in the period-finding; the factoring itself is number theory, and the engine shows exactly where the line is.

pre_run.summary: Shor's factorization of 15 verified on prod across two domains. quantum_computing: order of 2 mod 15 is r=4 (even), valid Shor period. number_theory: gcd(3,15)=3, gcd(5,15)=5, and 3,5 prime. 15 = 3 x 5, HOLDS 5/5, sealed. A false period (r=3) claimed valid BROKE. The engine verifies the result; it does not generate it.

domain_results.detail: order of 2 mod 15 is r=4 (2^4=16=1 mod 15, even): valid Shor period; extraction gives factors 3,5
false period r=3 (odd, 2^3=8 != 1 mod 15) claimed valid BROKE

wisdom: What looks like deep magic often has a plain seam running through it: the quantum machine does one hard thing -- find the hidden period -- and the rest is the oldest arithmetic, the splitting of a number into its primes. To search out that seam, to see exactly where the wonder ends and the checkable begins, is its own kind of reverence. 'It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter' (Proverbs 25:2). The factors were hidden in 15 from the beginning; the search only made them plain.

title: Grover's search -- square-root, not linear (and not magic)
situation: To find one marked item among N with no structure to exploit, a classical search needs about N/2 looks on average. Grover's quantum algorithm (1996) needs about T = floor(pi*sqrt(N)/4) -- the square root. The engine confirmed the optimal count across scales: N=4 -> 1, 16 -> 3, 64 -> 6, 256 -> 12, 1024 -> 25, and 1,000,000 -> 785. That last one is the picture to keep: a needle in a million-item haystack found in 785 steps, where a classical scan averages half a million. HOLDS 6/6, sealed. A wrong count (N=64 in 7 instead of 6) BROKE -- past the optimal, the probability of success actually falls, so more is not better.
use: Two honest lessons. First, the size of the win: square-root. A million becomes a thousand; a trillion becomes a million. Real, but QUADRATIC, not exponential -- Grover does NOT break encryption. (It is Shor's period-finding, a different algorithm, that threatens RSA; see located_shor_factors_15.) Doubling a key length restores the margin against Grover. Second, the discipline of the optimal stopping point: the success probability rises to a peak near T = floor(pi*sqrt(N)/4) and then FALLS if you keep going. The engine refuses an over-count, which is the quantum version of a plain truth -- there is a right number of times to look, and grinding past it loses what you had.

pre_run.summary: Grover's optimal iteration count verified on prod across six scales. T = floor(pi*sqrt(N)/4): N=4->1, 16->3, 64->6, 256->12, 1024->25, 1,000,000->785. HOLDS 6/6, sealed. A false count (N=64->7) BROKE. The quadratic (sqrt N) speedup, checked -- not the exponential one people assume. The engine verifies the count; it does not generate it.

domain_results.detail: false count N=64->7 BROKE (optimal is 6; past the peak success falls)

wisdom: Even the best search must still look -- 785 times for a million, not once and not never. The gift is not omniscience but a shorter, surer seeking, with a right number of attempts past which striving turns to loss. 'Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you' (Matthew 7:7). The promise is to the one who seeks the appointed way, not to the one who grinds past it.

title: Decoherence destroys quantum information faster than gates can run, without error correction
verification: T1/T2 times measure relaxation/dephasing. Superconducting qubits: ~100-300μs. Need ~1000 gate operations before decoherence dominates. Surface code can correct if physical error rate <1%.
wisdom: Useful fault-tolerant quantum computing needs ~10⁶ physical qubits for ~10³ logical qubits.

title: Google's 2019 'supremacy' was a contrived random-circuit task, not useful computation
verification: Sycamore 53-qubit: 200s for sampling task estimated at 10,000 years classically. Subsequent classical algorithms reduced classical estimate dramatically. Useful quantum advantage remains unproven.
wisdom: Distinguish 'quantum supremacy' demos from 'quantum advantage on useful problems' — the gap is large.

title: BB84 -- the eavesdropper cannot listen without leaving a trace
situation: BB84 (Bennett and Brassard, 1984) is how two people share a secret key over an open channel and KNOW whether anyone listened. The trick is physical: measuring a quantum bit disturbs it, so an eavesdropper who intercepts and re-sends necessarily injects errors. The two parties compare a sample of their bits and measure the quantum bit error rate (QBER). Below about 11% the key is safe to keep; at or above it, the channel is treated as compromised and the key is thrown away. The engine confirmed the rule across cases -- 2% and 9% secure, 11% and 15% not -- HOLDS 4/4, sealed. A false claim (20% error called secure) BROKE.
use: The lesson that travels beyond physics: BB84's security does not rest on the eavesdropper being too weak to break a cipher -- it rests on the fact that listening LEAVES A MARK that the honest parties can see and measure. The defense is not a harder lock but an unavoidable trace. Practically, QKD is real and deployed (banks, governments) and is one answer to the worry that future quantum computers will break today's public-key crypto (see located_shor_factors_15) -- it secures the key exchange with physics rather than with a math problem assumed hard. And the threshold is a discipline: when the error rate says you are watched, you do not push the secret through anyway -- you discard and start again.

pre_run.summary: BB84 QKD security threshold verified on prod. Secure iff QBER < 11% (Shor-Preskill bound): 2% and 9% secure, 11% and 15% not, HOLDS 4/4, sealed. A false label (20% error claimed secure) BROKE. The engine verifies the classification; it does not generate it.

domain_results.detail: QBER 2%/9% secure, 11%/15% compromised (threshold 11%); 4/4
false: QBER 20% claimed secure BROKE (above threshold)

wisdom: There is a kind of security that does not depend on the listener being weak, but on the truth that listening cannot be hidden: to intercept the message is to disturb it, and the disturbance testifies. So the honest two compare notes, see the trace, and know. 'For nothing is hidden that will not be made manifest, nor anything secret that will not be known and come to light' (Luke 8:17). What is done to the message in the dark does not stay in the dark.

title: NIST post-quantum standards picked lattice-based signatures and KEMs in 2024
verification: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM, Kyber), 204 (ML-DSA, Dilithium), 205 (SLH-DSA, SPHINCS+). Lattice problems (LWE, SIS) believed hard for quantum and classical. Migration ongoing through 2030.
wisdom: Plan crypto agility now; you'll be migrating from RSA/ECC for the next decade.

title: Quantum volume measures usable qubit count, not raw qubit count
verification: IBM's QV combines qubit count, connectivity, gate fidelity, error rates. Doubles per generation roughly. Raw qubit count alone is marketing; QV tells you what you can actually run.
wisdom: Compare quantum computers by quantum volume or algorithmic benchmark, not qubit count alone.

title: Below threshold (~1% per gate), surface code suppresses errors exponentially with code distance
verification: Fault-tolerance theorem: if physical error p < threshold pth, logical error scales as (p/pth)^(d/2). Modern superconducting qubits at ~0.1% physical, requiring d≈25 for 10⁻¹⁰ logical error.
wisdom: Each factor of 10 improvement in logical error costs ~2x the physical qubits.

title: Quantum annealers solve QUBO problems but are not universal
verification: D-Wave systems implement transverse-field Ising model evolution. Useful for optimization-shaped problems with QUBO formulation. No proven asymptotic speedup over classical heuristics in general.
wisdom: Annealers and gate-model quantum are different machines — match the architecture to the problem.

title: Iron-56 sits at the peak of nuclear binding energy per nucleon
verification: ~8.8 MeV/nucleon at Fe-56 (technically Ni-62 is slightly higher). Fusion below this releases energy; fission above this releases energy. Stars stop fusing at iron.
wisdom: Iron is where energy extraction stops — both directions cost energy past it.

title: Radioactive decay is exponential: N(t) = N₀ exp(-λt), half-life = ln(2)/λ
verification: Memoryless Poisson process; identical atoms have identical instantaneous decay probability. Macroscopic samples follow exponential law extremely precisely.
wisdom: Half-life applies to populations; you cannot predict when a specific atom decays.

title: Critical mass depends on geometry, purity, and reflector — not just isotope identity
verification: Bare sphere U-235: ~52 kg. With Be reflector: <20 kg. Spheres minimize surface-to-volume; reflectors return escaping neutrons. Demon core demonstrated geometry sensitivity tragically.
wisdom: 'Critical mass' is shorthand — actual criticality is a six-factor formula.

title: Light water reactors use water as both moderator and coolant
verification: Hydrogen nuclei have mass similar to neutrons, maximizing energy transfer per collision. Slows neutrons to thermal speeds for U-235 fission. Loss of coolant = loss of moderation in LWRs.
wisdom: This coupling is a safety feature: lose water, lose chain reaction.

title: Uranium-238 decay chain ends at stable Pb-206 via 14 intermediate steps
verification: Series of α and β⁻ decays; intermediate Rn-222 is the major source of natural radon. Half-life of parent U-238 is 4.47 Gyr — long enough to remain on Earth from solar system formation.
wisdom: Long half-lives mean primordial isotopes; short means anthropogenic or freshly produced.

title: Fission releases ~0.1% of mass-energy; chemical reactions release ~10⁻⁹
verification: U-235 fission yields ~200 MeV from ~235 GeV total rest mass = 8.5×10⁻⁴. Chemistry rearranges electrons (few eV per atom), ~10⁻⁹ of mass. Nuclear ~10⁶× chemical energy density.
wisdom: Energy density gap explains why nuclear and chemical fuels live in different universes.

title: Alpha stopped by paper, beta by aluminum, gamma needs lead
verification: α (helium nuclei) heavy/slow, large interaction cross section. β (electrons) lighter/faster. γ (photons) no charge, exponential attenuation. Penetration inversely correlates with biological damage per absorbed dose at the surface.
wisdom: External α is mostly harmless; internal α (Po-210, Ra-226) is devastating.

title: NIF achieved scientific breakeven (Q>1) in Dec 2022 — but engineering Q remains ~0.01
verification: 3.15 MJ fusion yield from 2.05 MJ laser energy delivered to target. Total electrical input to lasers was ~400 MJ. Useful net energy still distant.
wisdom: 'Breakeven' has multiple definitions — read which Q is reported.

title: Sun fuses hydrogen mostly via the proton-proton chain at its core temperature
verification: 15M K core; CNO cycle minor (~1%) in Sun, dominant in heavier stars. Each He-4 produced from 4 H releases 26.7 MeV. Sun fuses ~6.2×10¹¹ kg/s.
wisdom: Solar lifetime depends on H in core, not total H mass — only ~10% gets fused before main-sequence exit.

title: Chernobyl's RBMK had positive void coefficient — a design flaw, not just operator error
verification: Steam (void) in RBMK fuel channels increased reactivity rather than decreased it. Western LWRs have negative void coefficient by design — loss of water shuts down chain reaction.
wisdom: Safety lives in the physics, not just the procedures. Inherently safe designs are the foundation.

title: Triple point: a substance exists in solid, liquid, gas at one specific (T,P)
verification: Water's triple point: 273.16 K, 611.657 Pa. Defines the Kelvin (until 2019 redefinition). Below triple point, sublimation skips liquid phase entirely.
wisdom: Below triple pressure (Mars surface, vacuum chamber), water cannot be liquid at any temperature.

title: Above critical point, liquid and gas become indistinguishable: supercritical fluid
verification: Water Tc=647K, Pc=22.06 MPa. Supercritical CO₂ (304K, 7.4MPa) used as solvent in decaffeination, dry cleaning, oil extraction.
wisdom: Supercritical fluids combine gas-like diffusion with liquid-like density — useful and tunable.

title: First-order phase transitions release latent heat; second-order do not
verification: Melting, boiling = first-order, discontinuous in first derivative of free energy (entropy, volume). Ferromagnetic Curie point, superconductivity onset = second-order, continuous in entropy.
wisdom: Latent heat plateaus in temperature-vs-time curves — distinguish heating from phase change.

title: Eutectic composition melts at one temperature lower than either pure component
verification: Solder Sn63Pb37: melts at 183°C (eutectic) vs 232°C Sn / 327°C Pb. Single sharp melting point eases reflow. Lead-free SAC305 has higher eutectic (~217°C).
wisdom: Choose alloy composition near eutectic for sharp melting; off-eutectic gives mushy zone.

title: Clausius-Clapeyron: dP/dT = L/(T·ΔV) on a phase boundary
verification: For water vapor: ~7% saturation vapor pressure increase per °C warming. Drives heavier precipitation extremes in warming climate.
wisdom: 7% per degree is why floods intensify with warming, not just shift in location.

title: Water density peaks at 4°C — ice floats, lakes freeze top-down
verification: Hydrogen bonding forces tetrahedral structure at lower T. Maximum density 999.97 kg/m³ at 3.98°C. Lakes stratify with 4°C bottom water, ice on top — fish survive winter.
wisdom: Most substances are densest as solids; water is the famous exception.

title: Glass transition is a kinetic phenomenon, not a true thermodynamic phase transition
verification: Tg depends on cooling rate (faster cooling = higher Tg). No latent heat, no symmetry change. Amorphous solids are arrested liquids.
wisdom: Glass is not a slow liquid at room temperature — that's a myth from sagging cathedral windows (which were uneven when made).

title: Type-II superconductors expel magnetic flux up to a critical field, then form vortices
verification: Below Tc and Hc1, perfect Meissner expulsion. Between Hc1 and Hc2, flux penetrates as quantized vortices. Most practical superconductors (NbTi, YBCO) are Type-II.
wisdom: Pinning of vortices determines critical current — pure crystals carry less than impurity-pinned ones.

title: Metamorphic facies indicate the P-T conditions a rock survived
verification: Mineral assemblage (e.g., blueschist vs greenschist vs eclogite) maps to specific P-T windows. Index minerals (chlorite, biotite, garnet, staurolite, kyanite, sillimanite) order by grade.
wisdom: Rocks are thermometers and barometers of their geological history.

title: Gibbs phase rule: F = C - P + 2 gives degrees of freedom
verification: For a one-component system at triple point (P=3): F=0. For two-phase coexistence (P=2): F=1, a curve. Single phase: F=2, an area. Three-phase invariant point in binary system at fixed pressure.
wisdom: Counts the independent variables you can tune without changing the phase makeup.

title: Exposure depends on aperture × shutter × ISO, each in stops
verification: Doubling sensor light: open aperture one stop, lengthen shutter one stop, or raise ISO one stop. All three trade off something: depth of field, motion blur, noise.
wisdom: Stops are log scales — equal stops mean equal factors of 2 in light.

title: Depth of field is bounded by the circle of confusion, not infinitely sharp planes
verification: Acceptable sharpness defined by CoC (typically 0.03mm for 35mm). Hyperfocal distance focuses such that everything from hyperfocal/2 to infinity is acceptable.
wisdom: DoF charts assume a print size and viewing distance; pixel-peeping demands tighter CoC.

title: Flash illumination falls off as 1/distance² — a universal photographic constraint
verification: Point source spreads over expanding sphere. Doubling distance quarters illumination. Modify by reflection, diffusion, or beam shaping (softboxes, fresnel) but the law holds for the source itself.
wisdom: Bring the light closer rather than dialing up power — the falloff makes shadows ratio better too.

title: Closing aperture beyond f/8-f/11 softens images via diffraction
verification: Airy disk diameter ~2.44·λ·f-number. Above diffraction-limited aperture, resolution loses faster than depth of field gains. Crop sensors hit it sooner.
wisdom: Sweet spot for sharpness is often 2-3 stops down from wide open, not maximum aperture.

title: Color temperature describes black-body spectrum in Kelvin
verification: Daylight ~5500K, tungsten ~3200K, candle ~1850K. White balance shifts to neutralize. LEDs and fluorescents have discontinuous spectra approximated by correlated color temperature.
wisdom: CCT doesn't capture full spectrum — high CRI sources reproduce colors better at the same CCT.

title: Rule of thirds is a heuristic, not a compositional law
verification: Placing subjects on third-lines is one common arrangement. Centered subjects (symmetry), golden ratio, diagonals, leading lines all work. Composition follows attention, not grids.
wisdom: Start with thirds, break the rule when the subject demands it.

title: Image sensor noise: read noise + shot noise + dark current
verification: Shot noise = sqrt(photon count) — fundamental Poisson. Read noise dominates at low signal; shot noise at high signal. Cooling reduces dark current. Larger pixels collect more photons per area.
wisdom: At base ISO with bright light, you're shot-noise limited and SNR scales as sqrt(N).

title: Capitalization rate = NOI / property value — yield not return
verification: Cap rate excludes financing, depreciation, and capital appreciation. Compare like markets (urban vs suburban, asset class). Lower cap = higher price/income or expected growth.
wisdom: Cap rate is a snapshot — IRR captures the full hold period story.

title: USPAP requires sales comparison, cost, and income approaches when applicable
verification: Residential typically leans on sales comparison. Commercial weights income approach. Cost approach (replacement minus depreciation plus land) is the floor and matters for new construction or special-use.
wisdom: Three approaches converge in healthy markets; divergence signals distortion.

title: LTV measures leverage; CLTV adds second-position debt
verification: Conventional residential limit typically 80% before PMI. Above 95% triggers higher rates and insurance. Commercial typically 65-75% on stabilized assets.
wisdom: LTV underestimates risk when prices fall — equity erodes from the top of the stack.

title: 1031 exchange defers capital gains tax on like-kind real property
verification: IRC §1031: 45-day identification, 180-day closing, like-kind requirement (now real-property only after 2017 TCJA). Qualified intermediary required.
wisdom: Deferral is not forgiveness — basis carries over. Stepped-up basis at death extinguishes the deferred gain.

title: Zoning restricts supply; housing prices reflect the constraint
verification: Glaeser-Gyourko and others: cities with tighter zoning show housing prices well above construction cost. San Francisco's ratio is ~3x. Permissive Houston tracks construction cost more closely.
wisdom: Affordability is a supply problem in restricted markets, regardless of demand-side subsidies.

title: Property tax assessment is market value × assessment ratio × mill rate
verification: Mill = 1/1000 of value. Assessment ratios vary (some states full value, others 50-70%). Caps (CA Prop 13, TX 10%) constrain growth rate of assessed value.
wisdom: Compare effective rate (tax / market value), not nominal mill rate — assessment differences hide real burden.

title: DSCR = NOI / debt service; lenders require ≥1.20-1.25 for commercial
verification: Debt service coverage ratio measures cushion above mortgage payment. Below 1.0 = negative cash flow. Multifamily often 1.20, retail/office 1.25-1.30, hospitality higher.
wisdom: DSCR underwriting bites in rising-rate environments — coverage compresses fast.

title: Gross rent multiplier (GRM) is a quick screen, not a valuation method
verification: GRM = price / annual gross rent. Ignores expenses, vacancy, capex. Useful for back-of-envelope comparisons within a market segment, misleading across segments.
wisdom: Two properties at same GRM can have wildly different cap rates depending on expense load.

title: Fair Housing Act forbids discrimination by 7 protected classes
verification: Title VIII of Civil Rights Act 1968 + 1988 amendments: race, color, religion, national origin, sex, familial status, disability. Applies to sales, rentals, lending, advertising.
wisdom: Even neutral-looking criteria with disparate impact can violate — consult counsel before tightening application standards.

title: Lease-vs-own breakeven depends on horizon, transaction costs, and price-to-rent
verification: P/R ratios above ~20 favor renting; below ~15 favor owning. Transaction costs (5-7% sell side) require ~3-5 year hold to amortize. Tax treatment (mortgage interest, SALT cap) shifts the line.
wisdom: Short-horizon ownership rarely pencils once transaction costs are honest.

title: Title insurance is one-time premium for warranty against unknown title defects
verification: Owner's policy protects up to purchase price; lender's policy protects loan amount. Pays for legal defense and loss from prior liens, fraud, missing heirs not caught in title search.
wisdom: In high-risk states (Florida, Texas), the owner's policy is worth the one-time cost.

title: BRRRR (Buy-Rehab-Rent-Refinance-Repeat) only works in appreciating cap-rate-compressing markets
verification: Strategy relies on forced appreciation (rehab) plus refi at higher value to extract capital. Works when ARV > 1.25× all-in. Fails when interest rates rise or rehab costs exceed appraisal lift.
wisdom: BRRRR pencils in zero-interest era; underwrites tightly in normal rate environment.

title: WAR (Wins Above Replacement) is a composite metric with multiple incompatible implementations
verification: fWAR (FanGraphs) and bWAR (Baseball-Reference) use different defensive metrics, run environments, replacement levels. Can disagree by 2+ wins on same player.
wisdom: Quote both fWAR and bWAR; their disagreement is signal about uncertainty.

title: Hot streaks regress; cold streaks regress — selection bias drives both narratives
verification: Performance = true skill + noise. Extreme performance windows contain more noise than average. Selecting players on extreme months guarantees regression in subsequent months.
wisdom: Pay for true skill, not for recent variance.

title: Pythagorean expectation: Wins ≈ RS²/(RS²+RA²) — base 1.83 in baseball
verification: Bill James 1980; calibrated exponent ~1.83 for MLB. NFL ~2.37, NBA ~14. Teams over/underperforming Pythagorean by >5 games tend to regress.
wisdom: Pythagorean wins predict next season better than actual wins — luck dissipates.

title: In American football, expected points from a drive scale nearly linearly with starting field position
verification: Romer (2006), updated by nflscrapR data: ~0.04 EP per yard of field position. Starting at own 20 vs opponent's 20 differs by ~3 expected points.
wisdom: Punting from midfield often gives away more EP than fourth-down attempts gain.

title: Expected goals (xG) measures shot quality; xG over/under-performance regresses
verification: xG models score shots by historical conversion rate at angle/distance/defender position. Strikers consistently outperforming xG over small samples regress; long-run xG ≈ goals.
wisdom: Trust xG over goals after small samples; trust goals after a season.

title: Effective FG% weights 3-pointers properly: eFG% = (FGM + 0.5*3PM) / FGA
verification: Three-pointer worth 1.5× a two, so 0.5 bonus reflects extra value. Raw FG% understates volume three-point shooters. TS% adds free throws.
wisdom: Compare shooters by eFG% or TS%, not raw FG%.

title: DVOA adjusts for opponent strength and situation, not raw yards
verification: Football Outsiders DVOA: each play graded vs league average for down/distance/quarter/score, weighted by opponent strength. Predicts future wins better than yards.
wisdom: Yards-per-game is a poor team comparison — DVOA adjusts for context.

title: Even-paced marathons outperform positive splits for ~95% of runners
verification: Marathon data shows positive split (slower second half) by 5%+ is the modal pattern. World records typically negative split. Glycogen depletion math favors conservative early pace.
wisdom: Run the first half by feel; run the second half by what's left.

title: Home field advantage is real but smaller than perception — ~3 points in NFL, 0.5 goals in soccer
verification: COVID empty-stadium data confirmed referee bias contributes substantially. With crowds absent, home advantage in EPL dropped from ~0.4 to ~0.2 goals.
wisdom: Crowd influences officials more than players — quantified by natural experiment.

title: Most measured 'clutch' performance does not exceed sample variance
verification: Multiple studies (NBA, MLB) find clutch performance largely indistinguishable from random variation around season averages. A few outliers exist but most named 'clutch' players show no signal.
wisdom: Be skeptical of clutch narratives — small high-leverage samples produce stories from noise.

title: Aging curves peak in late 20s for most sports, decline thereafter
verification: Baseball hitters peak ~27, pitchers ~26. NBA peak ~27. Marathon peak ~32-35 (endurance advantage). Selection bias: only the late-career stars are visible because the rest washed out.
wisdom: Younger players have higher ceiling and more downside; price both.

title: A 95% CI does not mean '95% chance the parameter is in this interval'
verification: Frequentist CI: '95% of CIs constructed this way contain the true value across hypothetical repeated experiments'. The parameter is either in this specific CI or not — no probability.
wisdom: For 'probability parameter is in interval', you want a Bayesian credible interval, not a CI.

title: CI width scales as 1/sqrt(n) — halving width needs 4x sample
verification: For mean of iid sample: SE = σ/sqrt(n). 95% CI width ≈ 4σ/sqrt(n). Diminishing returns on sample size.
wisdom: From 100 to 400 samples: half the CI width. From 400 to 1600: half again. Plan accordingly.

title: Bootstrap CIs work for arbitrary statistics without distributional assumptions
verification: Resample with replacement B times, compute statistic each time, take 2.5th/97.5th percentiles. BCa correction handles bias. Works for medians, ratios, complex estimators.
wisdom: Bootstrap rescues you when the analytical CI formula is wrong or missing.

title: Overlapping 95% CIs do not imply 'not statistically different'
verification: Two means can have overlapping 95% CIs and still be different at p<0.05 — the comparison CI on the difference is shorter than either marginal. Schenker-Gentleman approximation: ~83% CIs work for visual comparison.
wisdom: Compute the difference's CI, not eyeball the marginal CIs.

title: CI coverage drops sharply when the assumed distribution is wrong
verification: t-interval on skewed data with n=20 can have 85% actual coverage instead of 95%. Bootstrap and robust methods help but are not free. Check residual diagnostics before trusting CI.
wisdom: Nominal coverage ≠ actual coverage when assumptions break.

title: OSI seven layers is a teaching model; real protocols don't map cleanly
verification: Physical/Data Link/Network/Transport/Session/Presentation/Application. TCP/IP collapses to four layers in practice. TLS straddles session+presentation. Naming layers helps debugging conversation; expecting code structure to follow it does not.
wisdom: Use OSI to locate where a problem lives, not to demand a tidy implementation.

title: TCP three-way handshake costs one round-trip before any data flows
verification: SYN -> SYN/ACK -> ACK. TLS adds another 1-2 RTTs (TLS 1.3 reduces to 1-RTT or 0-RTT). QUIC merges transport+crypto handshake into one RTT.
wisdom: For first-byte latency over long links, the handshake is often more expensive than the payload itself.

title: Bandwidth-delay product sets the buffer size for full-throughput TCP
verification: Window must hold one RTT worth of data: BDP = bandwidth * RTT. 100 Mb/s * 100ms = ~1.25MB. Default 64KB TCP windows can't fill long fat networks without scaling option.
wisdom: Slow long-distance transfers are usually window-size bottlenecked, not bandwidth bottlenecked.

title: Bufferbloat: oversized buffers cause latency, not the bandwidth they were meant to protect
verification: Jim Gettys 2010: excessive buffers absorb congestion signal, hiding it from TCP. Result: seconds of queueing delay on home routers under load. CoDel and FQ-CoDel solved it.
wisdom: More buffer is not more throughput; it's more latency under load.

title: CAP theorem: under partition, choose consistency or availability — not both
verification: Brewer 2000, formalized by Gilbert-Lynch 2002. Real systems live on PACELC: partition behavior, AND latency-consistency tradeoff outside partition. Strict CAP is an idealization.
wisdom: All distributed databases make this tradeoff somewhere — read the docs to find where.

title: DNS caching is essential but TTLs are honored only as suggestions
verification: Recursive resolvers, OS stub resolvers, browsers, and app caches each apply their own policies. Real expiration can exceed TTL by minutes to hours. Critical to remember during DNS failover.
wisdom: Plan for long TTL propagation regardless of what your zone says; pre-stage changes.

title: BGP trust model assumes neighbors are honest — RPKI is the partial fix
verification: BGP announces routes peer-to-peer with no built-in authentication. Route leaks and hijacks (Pakistan/YouTube 2008, Russia/Mastercard 2017) come from policy or compromise. RPKI cryptographically validates origin AS but not full path.
wisdom: BGP security is still mostly social and reputational; technical fixes are partial.

title: QUIC runs over UDP to bypass middlebox ossification
verification: Built on UDP because middleboxes block or mangle new TCP options. Native multiplexing, 0-RTT resumption, encrypted transport headers. HTTP/3 uses QUIC.
wisdom: QUIC also moves transport into userspace where iteration is faster than kernel TCP.

title: Film exposure below ~1 second loses linearity (reciprocity failure)
verification: Long exposures require more time than linear extrapolation suggests. Schwarzschild's law: corrected_time = nominal_time^p where p typically 1.2-1.4. Digital sensors do not suffer this — different bias structure (dark current).
wisdom: Astrophotography on film required big multipliers for hours-long exposures.

title: Modern sensors capture 12-14 stops of dynamic range; print can show only 7-9
verification: FF CMOS sensors at base ISO measure ~14 EV scene contrast. Print substrate limits Dmax to ~7 stops; modern HDR displays push toward 10. Tone mapping bridges the gap.
wisdom: Don't blow highlights at capture; the headroom is gone before output.

title: Hyperfocal distance H = f²/(N*c) where c is circle of confusion
verification: Focusing at H makes everything from H/2 to infinity acceptably sharp. Standard 35mm at f/8 with c=0.03mm: H ~ 17m for 50mm lens. Wide lenses have much shorter H.
wisdom: Landscape photographers focus at hyperfocal for maximum DoF, then check sharpness at H/2.

title: Chromatic aberration: refractive index varies with wavelength; lens elements compensate
verification: Longitudinal CA shifts focal plane by color; lateral CA shifts position across frame. Apochromat designs combine glass types (ED, fluorite) to bring three wavelengths to a common focus.
wisdom: Cheap fast lenses show CA worst at high contrast edges; software correction is partial.

title: RAW preserves sensor data; JPEG bakes the camera's interpretation
verification: RAW = 12-14 bit per channel, no demosaic, no WB, no compression artifacts. JPEG = 8-bit + chroma subsampling + lossy DCT. Post-processing latitude is dramatically different.
wisdom: Shoot RAW when you might edit; JPEG when you won't.

title: PWR keeps primary loop pressurized to prevent boiling; BWR boils in core directly
verification: PWR: primary loop ~155 bar, 320°C, no boiling; secondary loop generates steam via steam generator. BWR: water boils in core, steam directly to turbine. Most US reactors are PWR (~65%) or BWR (~35%).
wisdom: PWR isolates radioactive primary; BWR is simpler but turbine becomes contaminated.

title: Breeder reactors produce more fissile material than they consume
verification: Fast neutrons converting U-238 to Pu-239 at rate > consumption (breeding ratio > 1). Demonstrated in Superphenix, BN-600, EBR-II. Commercial deployment limited by cost, proliferation concerns, and abundant uranium.
wisdom: Breeders could extend fissile resources ~100x, but the economics have rarely justified deployment.

title: Pure water can supercool to -40°C without ice nucleation
verification: Homogeneous nucleation barrier requires extreme purity and stillness. Cloud droplets supercool to -38°C routinely. Adding nucleation site (dust, agitation) triggers immediate freezing.
wisdom: Freezing point is a kinetic threshold, not just a thermodynamic one.

title: Martensitic transformation is diffusionless — shear, not migration
verification: Rapid quench of steel traps carbon; FCC austenite shears to BCT martensite by coordinated lattice distortion. No atom diffuses far. Origin of steel hardening since the Iron Age.
wisdom: Slow cooling lets carbon diffuse out; fast cooling locks it in and hardens steel.

title: Soil profile horizons (O,A,E,B,C,R) reflect weathering and biology, not deposition
verification: O: organic litter. A: topsoil (humus mixed). E: eluviated/leached. B: subsoil (illuvial accumulation). C: parent material. R: bedrock. Develops over centuries to millennia.
wisdom: Topsoil is a developmental product, not a geological layer — losing it can take centuries to rebuild.

title: Each 1% increase in soil organic matter holds an additional ~20,000 gallons/acre of water
verification: Estimate from USDA-NRCS: OM increases plant-available water capacity ~3.7%/percent OM. Magnitude varies by texture; sands gain more relative capacity than clays.
wisdom: Building OM is drought insurance; conventional tillage is the fastest way to lose it.

title: Chiasmus (ABBA pattern) anchors memory and emphasizes inversion
verification: Greek X-shape: 'ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.' Hebrew scripture uses extended chiastic structure (e.g., Gen 11 tower) at large scale.
wisdom: Chiasmus is a memory and structure device used long before printing.

title: Tonal music's tension-release pivots on dominant-tonic motion
verification: V chord contains the leading tone (7th degree) that resolves up to tonic. Cadential motion V-I underlies most Western harmony 1600-1900. Authentic, plagal, deceptive, half — varieties of expected vs. surprised resolution.
wisdom: If the music wants to move, follow the V; if it's resting, you're at I.

title: Kerckhoffs's principle: security depends on key secrecy, not algorithm secrecy
verification: 1883 essay; restated by Shannon as 'the enemy knows the system'. Public algorithms get peer-reviewed and stress-tested; secret algorithms are usually weak (security through obscurity).
wisdom: If your encryption depends on the attacker not knowing how it works, it's already broken.

title: MLB home run rates spiked 1994-2005 well above historical variance
verification: Per-game HR rate jumped ~40% above 1980s baseline; multiple records (single-season HR, season SLG) fell. Subsequent PED testing and ball-composition changes reverted rates toward historical mean.
wisdom: Sports records have eras; compare within era or adjust for environment.

title: Default-off notifications outperform default-on across every measured cognitive task
verification: Multiple replication studies (Stothart 2015, Mark 2017): even silent notifications degrade focused performance by ~20-30% via cue-detection cost. Default-on assumes urgency; default-off requires intent to be reached.
wisdom: Turn it all off, then turn on what you actually need. The audit by default is to nothing.

title: MFA stops 99%+ of bulk credential-theft attacks; phishing-resistant MFA stops the rest
verification: Microsoft 2019 study: 99.9% reduction in account-compromise rate from MFA. FIDO2/passkeys resist real-time phishing because the credential is bound to the origin.
wisdom: Even SMS MFA is night-and-day better than password-only. The right MFA (passkey) closes the last percent.

title: Credit card grace period applies only to new purchases when prior balance is paid in full
verification: Federal Reserve Regulation Z: 21-day grace period available on purchases if statement balance was paid in full the prior month. Once you carry any balance, new purchases accrue interest from the transaction date.
wisdom: Carry a balance once and the interest starts on every new swipe immediately — pay in full or pay interest from the moment you bought it.

title: Three to six months of expenses is the conventional emergency-fund target
verification: The 3-6 month rule is heuristic, not empirical law. Single-income households, contract workers, and households with dependents often need 6-12. Dual-income stable households can do less if both incomes are unlikely to fail simultaneously.
wisdom: Rule of thumb, not law — adjust by income volatility and dependent count.

title: Rule of 72: years to double = 72 / annual return percent
verification: Mathematical approximation. At 7% annual return: 72/7 ≈ 10.3 years to double. Exact answer is ln(2)/ln(1.07) ≈ 10.24 years. Accurate within ~1% for rates from 1% to 15%.
wisdom: Memorize the formula and you can do compound interest in your head. At 10% money doubles in 7 years; at 6% in 12.

title: Sleep timing consistency predicts metabolic health better than total sleep duration
verification: Sleep regularity index (SRI) studies (Phillips 2017, Lunsford-Avery 2018): irregular sleep timing predicts mortality, diabetes, cardiovascular events independently of total hours. Same time every night beats more hours unevenly.
wisdom: Same bedtime > more hours of mixed bedtime. The body wants regularity more than it wants extra hours.

title: Urine color predicts hydration status better than ounces consumed
verification: Validated urine color charts (Armstrong 1994 et seq.): pale-straw color indicates adequate hydration; dark yellow indicates deficit. The 8-glasses-a-day rule has no empirical basis — needs vary by mass, activity, climate.
wisdom: Output, not input. Pale-yellow is the target; dark means drink. Forget the eight glasses.

title: Adult protein RDA (0.8 g/kg) is a floor, not a target; aging adults need ~1.2-1.6
verification: RDA was set to prevent deficiency, not optimize. Sarcopenia research (Bauer 2013, Phillips 2016) supports 1.2-1.6 g/kg for adults over 50 to maintain muscle mass. Strength athletes function well at 1.6-2.2.
wisdom: The RDA prevents disease. Aging adults need more to keep muscle. Lean muscle is the metabolic engine.

title: 7,000 steps/day captures most mortality benefit; 10,000 is a marketing number
verification: Paluch 2022 meta-analysis: mortality risk drops steeply from sedentary to ~7,000 steps, then plateaus. 10,000 originated as a 1965 Japanese pedometer marketing campaign (manpo-kei = '10,000 steps meter').
wisdom: 7,000 is the dose-response point. 10,000 is a brand from 1965. Don't quit at 8,000 because you're not at 10K.

title: Healthy in-law relationships depend on the spouse mediating their own parents
verification: Family systems research (Bowen, Murray): in-law conflict almost always resolves better when each spouse handles their own family of origin rather than the other handling theirs. Boundary-from-the-source vs. boundary-via-spouse.
wisdom: Your parents are your job; their parents are theirs. Boundary through the blood line, not across the marriage.

title: Blended family integration averages 5-7 years; first 2 years are hardest
verification: Patricia Papernow's stepfamily research: integration follows predictable stages (fantasy, immersion, awareness, mobilization, action, contact, resolution) over 4-12 years. Most stepfamily distress occurs in the awareness-mobilization phase.
wisdom: The first two years are the awareness phase; pain there is normal, not failure. Hold the long arc.

title: Toddler screen time correlates with delayed language; effect concentrates under age 3
verification: Madigan 2019, AAP guidelines: under 18 months — none beyond video calls; 18-24 months — high-quality co-viewed only; 2-5 years — under 1 hour/day. Causality runs through reduced caregiver-child verbal interaction.
wisdom: The screen is not the only cost; the conversation it displaced is.

title: A real apology has three parts: acknowledgment, ownership, and changed behavior
verification: Lazare's apology research: 'I'm sorry you feel that way' lacks ownership; 'I'm sorry I did it' lacks change. The full apology names what happened, takes the blame, and commits to (then does) different.
wisdom: Apology = acknowledge + own + change. Missing any one leaves the wound open.

title: Decision quality degrades over a day of accumulated choices
verification: Original Danziger 2011 parole study has been contested (Glöckner 2016). The mechanism — ego depletion — is also weak after replication failures. But the practical pattern (choice quality drops with fatigue, regardless of mechanism) holds in observational data.
wisdom: Mechanism contested, pattern observed: make important decisions early and well-fed. Don't ask the tired you to pick.

title: Sunk cost cannot be recovered; only marginal costs and benefits matter
verification: Standard rationality result. Continuing because 'I've already invested X' is a fallacy — X is gone either way. Only the next dollar/hour/decision has agency.
wisdom: Your past investment doesn't vote on the next decision. Only what comes next does.

title: Calendar invites with default-accept settings inflate meeting load by 30-50%
verification: Microsoft, Atlassian internal studies: turning calendar default from auto-accept to no-response cuts meeting count substantially. The asymmetry — easy to invite, hard to decline — concentrates on the recipient.
wisdom: Default-decline; only accept on intent. The friction belongs on the inviter, not the invitee.

title: Inbox-zero as productivity goal is debunked; threshold-zero (e.g. <50) is sustainable
verification: Mark Hurst's original inbox-zero meant zero ATTENTION, not zero emails. The literal interpretation became unsustainable for high-volume roles. Modern guidance: triage to a threshold (e.g. 50), batch process 2-3x/day, archive aggressively.
wisdom: Inbox-zero was a misread. The goal is no email holding attention, not no email present.

title: Planning fallacy: people underestimate own task duration by ~30-50% on average
verification: Kahneman-Tversky 1979 et seq.: even adjusting for past overruns, people repeat the mistake. Reference-class forecasting (looking at how long similar tasks actually took) reduces the bias substantially.
wisdom: Estimate, then add 50%. Or better, find a comparable past task and quote its actual duration.

title: Quoting prior text in a reply reduces miscommunication by ~40% in group chats
verification: User-studies on group chat platforms (Slack, Teams, Discord research). When threading or quoting is enabled and used, context-loss errors drop. Group chats without quoting see frequent 'wait, which thing?' confusions.
wisdom: Quote what you're replying to. The 200ms of friction saves the 5-minute follow-up clarification.

title: Overlearning to ~120% before testing produces durable retention
verification: Driskell 1992 meta-analysis: practice beyond initial mastery (overlearning) significantly improves long-term retention. Diminishing returns past ~120-150% of mastery threshold.
wisdom: Drill past 'I got it.' The first time you got it isn't the last time you'll need it.

title: Single-tasking outperforms multitasking on nearly every measured cognitive task
verification: Ophir 2009, Strayer multitasking lab: heavy multitaskers perform worse on every component task than focused workers. The exception is true automated background work (e.g. walking + talking). Cognitive task + cognitive task degrades both.
wisdom: You aren't multitasking; you're switching, and switching is expensive. One thing until it's done, then the next.

title: First minute of a meeting predicts the rest — set the question, not the agenda
verification: Behavioral studies on meeting effectiveness (Allen 2014): meetings that open with a clear question outperform meetings that open with a list of topics. The question creates focus; the list disperses it.
wisdom: Open with 'what are we deciding today?' not 'here are seven topics.' First minute sets the rest.

title: Two-pizza teams (≤8 people) outperform larger teams on communication-bound work
verification: Brooks' Law origin + Bezos' Amazon principle: communication overhead grows O(n²) with team size. At ~8 people, time-in-meeting starts to dominate time-doing. Splitting beats growing past this threshold.
wisdom: Two-pizza max. Beyond eight, the meeting becomes the work.

title: Most knowledge workers can sustain 3-4 hours of true deep work per day
verification: Ericsson's expert-practice studies, Cal Newport's surveys of knowledge workers. Beyond ~4 hours, focus quality degrades sharply. The variance is high, and 'true deep work' is poorly measured outside research contexts.
wisdom: Plan for ~4 hours of real focus, not 8. The other hours will be there for shallow work whether you plan for them or not.

title: Daily expressive writing (15-20 min) reliably reduces rumination across studies
verification: Pennebaker's expressive-writing paradigm (1986+): daily structured writing about stressors produces sustained psychological benefit, particularly for rumination and intrusive thoughts. Effect sizes are modest but durable.
wisdom: Write it down to stop it from running in your head. Same content; different storage location.

title: Bus factor of 1 is a single point of failure, not a productivity feature
verification: Software-team risk literature: any knowledge held by exactly one person is a structural fragility. The minimum healthy team bus factor is 2; institutional knowledge under 3 carries audit risk for compliance frameworks.
wisdom: If only one person can do it, that person is the bottleneck and the failure mode.

title: Test-driven development pays ~15-30% upfront for ~50% maintenance discount
verification: Multiple TDD studies (Williams 2003, Nagappan 2008): initial velocity drops 15-35% but defect rate falls 40-90% and maintenance time reduces sharply. Effect is largest on long-lived codebases; weakest on prototype/throwaway code.
wisdom: TDD pays back over time, not in week one. Apply where the code will outlive a year.

title: YAGNI: 'you aren't gonna need it' — speculative generality costs more than refactor
verification: Empirical and folk-empirical principle from XP. Code written 'for future needs' has ~60% never-used rate per Beck's reports. Refactor cost when the need actually arrives is typically lower than the up-front speculative-generality cost.
wisdom: Build the small thing now. The big thing you imagine is usually wrong.

title: Highway throughput peaks at ~75% capacity; jams form above that
verification: Traffic flow theory (Greenshields, Daganzo): vehicle flux is maximized at ~75% of jam density. Above that, small perturbations propagate backward as shockwaves; below, traffic stays free-flowing.
wisdom: Adding cars past ~75% capacity reduces total throughput. Phantom jams are an emergent property of saturation, not driver behavior.

title: Lithium-ion battery health degrades fastest at 100% SoC and high temperature
verification: Calendar aging models (Wang 2014, Schmalstieg 2014): time-at-100%-SoC and elevated temperature dominate cycle-independent capacity loss. Storing at 20-80% extends usable life 2-3×.
wisdom: Keep lithium cells between 20-80% when possible. The 'top-off charge' overnight is shortening your battery.

title: Consumer SSD endurance is typically 600 TBW; that's decades for normal users
verification: Manufacturer TBW specs (Samsung 870 EVO, Crucial MX series): 600-1200 terabytes written for consumer NVMe/SATA SSDs. Normal home writes are 5-20 GB/day; pro workloads 50-100 GB/day. Most users hit obsolescence before endurance.
wisdom: SSD wear isn't a thing for most users. The drive will be obsolete before it dies.

title: DNS propagation is mostly TTL expiration, not 'replication'
verification: DNS standards (RFC 1035): there is no 'propagation'; each resolver caches by TTL. Slow DNS changes are old caches still serving the prior record. Pre-shorten TTL hours before a change to make rollover fast.
wisdom: DNS doesn't propagate. It caches. Lower the TTL before changing records, not after.

title: TLS 1.0/1.1 are end-of-life; 1.2 is minimum, 1.3 preferred
verification: RFC 8996 (2021) formally deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1. PCI DSS 4.0, NIST 800-52r2, and all major browsers require ≥1.2. TLS 1.3 is faster (1-RTT or 0-RTT handshake) and removes weak ciphers entirely.
wisdom: If you're still terminating TLS 1.0/1.1, you're in violation of every modern compliance regime. 1.3 is the answer.

title: Random 4-word passphrases beat complex 12-char passwords on both entropy and recall
verification: XKCD 936; NIST SP 800-63b (2017). 4 random words from a 7,776-word list (Diceware) ≈ 51.7 bits entropy. Complex 12-char with mixed case + special chars ≈ 50-60 bits but is harder to recall. Passphrases win on usability without losing security.
wisdom: correct-horse-battery-staple beats P@ssw0rd!23. Length over complexity.

title: 3-2-1 backup rule: 3 copies, 2 different media, 1 off-site
verification: Long-standing industry rule (US-CERT, NIST SP 800-34). Withstands single-device failure, ransomware, and site-loss simultaneously. The 'untested backup' is the actual failure mode — restore drills convert a backup from theory into reality.
wisdom: Backups you haven't restored from are not backups. They're hope.

title: MERV-13 indoor air filtration captures most respiratory virus particles
verification: ASHRAE 241 (2023) recommends MERV-13 minimum for infectious aerosol mitigation. Captures ≥85% of 1-3 µm particles — the size range of respiratory droplet nuclei. Higher-rated HEPA captures ≥99.97% at 0.3 µm but at much higher airflow cost.
wisdom: MERV-13 in HVAC is high-impact, low-effort indoor air upgrade. HEPA where airflow allows.

title: Vitamin D deficiency is common in northern latitudes; supplementation is well-tolerated
verification: Endocrine Society guidelines: 25(OH)D <30 ng/mL is insufficient; <20 deficient. Trial evidence for supplementation outcomes is mixed (VITAL 2019 found no major mortality benefit), but correcting documented deficiency is safe and standard.
wisdom: Test, then supplement to correct deficiency. Don't dose blindly; don't fear correcting a real deficit.

title: Daily flossing is weakly supported by trials but professionally recommended
verification: Cochrane 2011 found weak evidence for floss in addition to brushing reducing gum disease. ADA still recommends because biological plausibility is high and harm is essentially zero. Brushing alone leaves ~40% of tooth surface untouched.
wisdom: Floss daily. The evidence is weak in trials but biology is strong, and the floor side (zero harm) is solid.

title: Zone 2 training builds aerobic base; most age-group athletes under-train it
verification: Maffetone, Seiler (polarized training): 80% of endurance training should be at low intensity (~65-75% max HR), 20% at high. Most age-group athletes train 'gray zone' at moderate intensity — too hard for aerobic gain, too easy for high-end stimulus.
wisdom: Easy days easy, hard days hard. The mistake is making the easy days too hard.

title: Resistance training has the largest evidence base of any intervention for aging healthspan
verification: Sarcopenia research, mortality cohort studies (Stamatakis 2018): grip strength and muscle mass independently predict mortality across age groups. Strength training 2x/week is the protocol with strongest causal evidence.
wisdom: Lift weights. Two days a week. The strongest cohort-evidence intervention against aging-related decline.

title: Body fat loss follows energy balance, but adherence drives outcomes more than diet composition
verification: Hall et al. metabolic ward studies confirm thermodynamic energy balance. Free-living trials (e.g., DIETFITS 2018) show diet composition (low-carb vs low-fat) doesn't differ at 12 months. Sustainable adherence is the variable that predicts outcomes.
wisdom: You can lose fat on any diet you can actually stick to. The composition is secondary; the adherence is primary.

title: Evening blue light suppresses melatonin; warm displays + reduced brightness help
verification: Chang 2015 (Harvard): screen reading delayed melatonin by 90 min vs print. F.lux/Night Shift studies show modest sleep-onset benefits when used 2+ hours before bed.
wisdom: Dim screens at sunset. The brightness setting is the most impactful lever.

title: Caffeine half-life is ~5 hours; afternoon coffee still affects sleep
verification: Caffeine pharmacokinetics: T½ 4-6 hours typical, longer in pregnancy and slow metabolizers. Drewnowski 2013: 200mg coffee 6h before bed reduces sleep efficiency. Genetic CYP1A2 variants double the variability.
wisdom: 2pm coffee is still 25% active at midnight for fast metabolizers; 50% for slow. Cut earlier than feels necessary.

title: Chronotype (morning/evening person) is ~50% heritable
verification: Jones 2019 GWAS (UK Biobank, n=697K): chronotype heritability ~46%, with PER, CRY, and ARNTL gene variants explaining major fraction. Schedule mismatch ('social jet lag') correlates with metabolic and cardiovascular risk.
